verdict.note: throwaway serve on isolated SPEXCODE_HOME store: active(genesis)→sed-written asking(note)→awaiting/merge(long note) all recorded in order with ISO ts + full notes + composed display words (working/asking/review); backend restart → byte-identical timeline (no dup genesis); post-restart transition still recorded; unknown id 404

A/reproduce: the read surface renders adjacent identical status lines verbatim — rig shows 4 dupes (working×2 ×2, asking×2, parked×2 identical notes); live :8787 session 314197a9 (the one the user saw two consecutive workings on) shows 3. Cause: a second serve (worktree/eval throwaway) observing the same store races the live recorder — each process's lastSeen appends the same record move once.

B/fix: readTimeline folds adjacent identical (status,proposal,note) runs at read time — same rig renders 0 dupes (was 4); all 17 distinct asking notes and 16 sent events survive untouched. Read-side fold also heals the already-polluted live histories (314197a9 etc.) with no data migration.
reproduced on main: plain human send after a note-send is delivered bare — no counter-signal, the note instruction persists unchallenged (the sticky-note complaint)
note→terminal transition now delivers the one-shot terminal-attached counter-insert; third send bare; timeline insert-free — verified on a real dispatched worker

codex harness, behavioral: natural Q&A probe answered the note-send via --note, flipped to normal terminal replies at the transition (note back to a short status line), stayed bare after, and the cycle re-enters — sticky-note behaviour gone on the worst-case harness
3 writers incl. raw sed all recorded in order with full notes + display words; restart appends nothing; unknown id 404 — re-measured on the fix branch
adjacent identical status runs fold to first; distinct-note neighbour and sent kept — re-measured on the fix branch
